name: nvim
description: Neovim - hyperextensible Vim-based text editor with Lua scripting, built-in LSP, and modern terminal features

globalOptions.name: --help
globalOptions.description: Show usage summary and exit

globalOptions.name: --version
globalOptions.description: Print version information and exit

globalOptions.name: -c
globalOptions.description: Execute an Ex command after loading the first file

globalOptions.name: --cmd
globalOptions.description: Execute an Ex command before loading any config

globalOptions.name: -S
globalOptions.description: Source (execute) a Vim/Lua script file after loading the first file

globalOptions.name: -u
globalOptions.description: Use specified init file instead of default init.vim/init.lua (NONE to skip)

globalOptions.name: -N
globalOptions.description: Start in nocompatible mode (always enabled in Neovim, accepted for compatibility)

globalOptions.name: -R
globalOptions.description: Read-only mode - set 'readonly' option

globalOptions.name: -b
globalOptions.description: Binary mode - set options for editing binary files

globalOptions.name: -d
globalOptions.description: Diff mode - open files and show differences

globalOptions.name: -o
globalOptions.description: Open N files in horizontally split windows

globalOptions.name: -O
globalOptions.description: Open N files in vertically split windows

globalOptions.name: -p
globalOptions.description: Open N files in separate tab pages

globalOptions.name: -r
globalOptions.description: Recovery mode - recover a file from swap file

globalOptions.name: -n
globalOptions.description: No swap file - do not create .swp files

globalOptions.name: -e
globalOptions.description: Start in Ex mode (line-oriented editing)

globalOptions.name: -s
globalOptions.description: Silent (batch) mode - read Normal mode commands from a file

globalOptions.name: --noplugin
globalOptions.description: Skip loading all plugins

globalOptions.name: --clean
globalOptions.description: Start with default settings, no config, no plugins (mimics factory defaults)

globalOptions.name: -t
globalOptions.description: Jump to a tag
Start at the end of the file

globalOptions.name: +N
globalOptions.description: Start at line number N

globalOptions.name: +/pattern
globalOptions.description: Start at the first occurrence of search pattern

globalOptions.name: --headless
globalOptions.description: Start without a UI (for scripting, LSP, automation)

globalOptions.name: --listen
globalOptions.description: Start an RPC server listening on the given address (pipe or TCP)

globalOptions.name: --remote
globalOptions.description: Edit files in an already running Neovim instance via --listen address

globalOptions.name: --embed
globalOptions.description: Use stdin/stdout as an msgpack-RPC channel (for GUI frontends)

globalOptions.name: --startuptime
globalOptions.description: Write startup timing messages to specified file

globalOptions.name: --api-info
globalOptions.description: Print msgpack-encoded API metadata to stdout and exit

globalOptions.name: -l
globalOptions.description: Execute a Lua script (non-editor mode, Neovim acts as Lua interpreter)

globalOptions.name: -ll
globalOptions.description: Execute a Lua module (like require('module'), non-editor mode)

globalOptions.name: -i
globalOptions.description: Use specified ShaDa (shared data) file instead of default

globalOptions.name: -V
globalOptions.description: Verbose mode - set verbosity level for debugging

globalOptions.name: -w
globalOptions.description: Record all typed characters to a script file (append)

globalOptions.name: -W
globalOptions.description: Record all typed characters to a script file (overwrite)

globalOptions.name: --luamod-dev
globalOptions.description: Enable Lua module development mode (add cwd to package.path)

examples.description: Start headless and quit (verify startup works)
Open two files in diff mode
Start Neovim as an RPC server on a Unix socket
Profile startup time and save to log
Execute a Lua script using Neovim as interpreter
Open all Python files in tabs
Open file and jump to line 42
Open file with no config or plugins
Open file with line numbers and a colorscheme
Open file in an already running Neovim instance
Execute a Lua module in non-editor mode

detectors.name: plugins
detectors.description: Detect plugin manager and installed plugins
